title: Gently Simmered Ethiopian Lamb Stew
description: A cultured and juicy Ethiopian dish featuring tender lamb and a rich, slightly thickened broth, made with kanten-agar and a deglazed fond, all Whole30-friendly and cooked to perfection on a comal.
instructions: Heat a comal over medium heat and add 2 tablespoons of oil to the center.
Add the chopped onion and cook until it is translucent and starting to caramelize.
Add the minced garlic and grated ginger and cook for 1 minute, stirring constantly.
Add the lamb cubes and cook until they are browned on all sides, about 5 minutes.
Add the berbere spice, salt, and black pepper, and stir to combine.
Add the beef broth and dissolved kanten-agar, and stir to combine.
Bring the mixture to a boil, then reduce the heat to low and simmer, covered, for 1 1/2 hours.
Remove the comal from the heat and let it cool slightly.
Deglaze the fond from the comal with a little water, scraping up any browned bits.
Stir the deglazed fond back into the stew and serve hot.
